{{Quote|Last night, 60 Arakyd AAD-4 assault droids entered Viper Tower just prior to the end of the business day quickly eliminating ground-floor security units.|"Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover"|<ref name="Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover" />}}
The '''AAD-4''' was a line of [[Battle droid/Legends|assault droids]] manufactured by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]. Hulking, [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]<ref name="AAD-4" /> [[Class four droid/Legends|class four droid]]s<ref name="TNEGTD" /> strong enough to lift a [[Vulptereen/Legends|Vulptereen]] off the ground by their neck, they had [[Color/Legends|bronze]] plating and were equipped with a pair of black [[Photoreceptor/Legends|photoreceptors]].<ref name="Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover" /> Shortly before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|outbreak]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|4|AAD-4}}</ref> in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]],<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Arakyd sent sixty AAD-4 assault [[Droid/Legends|droids]] into [[Viper Tower]], headquarters of [[Viper Sensor Intelligence Systems]],<ref name="Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover" /> during its hostile corporate takeover of VSIS,<ref name="CSWE" /> which quickly eliminated its ground floor security units. A [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] remained in order to assist with the transition.<ref name="Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover" />

==Behind the scenes==
The AAD-4 assault droid first appeared within the [[HoloNet News (original website)|HoloNet News website]] in the [[in-universe]] issue [[HoloNet [[HoloNet News Vol. 531 50|''HoloNet News Vol. 531'' 50]], published<ref name="Sensor Technology Dispute Settled in Takeover" /> [[April 4]], [[2002]].<ref name="HNN50 date">{{JCFCite|url=posts/6308795|board=Literature|thread=The Official HoloNetNews Discussion Thread|user=Mavrick889|date=2002-04-04|time=3:16 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200305125429/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-official-holonetnews-discussion-thread.5990764/page-13}}</ref> and written by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and [[Paul Ens]].<ref name="HNN Credits">{{HoloNetNewsWeb||credits.html|Credits}}</ref> Its design was derived from early [[Droideka/Legends|droideka]] concept art developed by [[Doug Chiang]] for the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']].<ref name="The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace]]''</ref>
